user_query:
I need you to generate four Git workflow artifacts from provided text inputs that simulate local Git CLI outputs. Read the files under input/ as described below and produce the outputs exactly as specified.

Inputs to read:
- input/commit_diff_stat.txt — treat as the exact output of: git diff --cached --stat
- input/commit_diff.txt — treat as the exact output of: git diff --cached
- input/current_branch.txt — the current branch name for the PR description
- input/base_branch.txt — the base branch name for the PR description
- input/pr_log_oneline.txt — treat as the exact output of: git log <base>..<branch> --oneline
- input/pr_diff_stat.txt — treat as the exact output of: git diff <base>..<branch> --stat
- input/changelog_log_oneline.txt — treat as the exact output of: git log --oneline -20 (or an equivalent range)
- input/branch_description.txt — a free-text description to turn into a branch name

What to produce (write all files under output/):
1) output/commit.json — JSON object that follows these rules:
   - Determine the changed file list by parsing input/commit_diff_stat.txt: collect every line that contains a pipe character "|"; take the file path as the part before "|", trimmed.
   - Compute type in this priority order:
     a) "test" if any filename contains "test" or "spec" (case-insensitive)
     b) "docs" if every filename is a Markdown file (*.md) or contains "readme" or "doc" (case-insensitive)
     c) "fix" if input/commit_diff.txt contains both a line starting with "+ " and a line starting with "- ", and the number of changed files is <= 3
     d) Otherwise, "feat"
   - Compute scope: if all files share the same top-level directory (segment before the first "/"), use that as scope; otherwise null.
   - Compute subject:
     - If exactly one file changed: "update <basename>" where basename is the filename without directories
     - If multiple files changed: "update N files (+A/-R)" where:
       - A is the count of lines in input/commit_diff.txt that start with "+" but not "++"
       - R is the count of lines in input/commit_diff.txt that start with "-" but not "--"
   - Compute message:
     - If scope is non-null: "<type>(<scope>): <subject>"
     - Else: "<type>: <subject>"
     - Truncate message to a maximum of 72 characters.
   - The JSON must include: { "message": string, "type": string, "scope": string|null, "files": number } where "files" is the number of changed files parsed from input/commit_diff_stat.txt.

2) output/pr.json — JSON object for a PR description generated as follows:
   - Read current branch from input/current_branch.txt and base branch from input/base_branch.txt (trim whitespace). Include both in the JSON as branch and baseBranch.
   - Title: take the current branch, replace all occurrences of "-", "_", and "/" with a single space, then remove a leading "feature ", "fix ", or "chore " if present (case-sensitive removal of exactly those words followed by a space). The rest of the title remains unchanged.
   - Commits: count the non-empty lines in input/pr_log_oneline.txt and store as "commits" (number).
   - Summary: for each non-empty line in input/pr_log_oneline.txt, take the substring starting at character index 8 (i.e., drop the first 8 characters, assuming 7-char hash and a space) and prefix with "- ", joining entries with a single "\n" newline between items.
   - filesChanged: count the number of lines in input/pr_diff_stat.txt that contain a pipe character "|".
   - The JSON must include: { "branch": string, "baseBranch": string, "title": string, "commits": number, "summary": string, "filesChanged": number }.

3) output/changelog.json — JSON object grouped by commit type from input/changelog_log_oneline.txt:
   - For each non-empty line, take the substring starting at character index 8 (drop the first 8 characters as above) as the message.
   - Group messages into arrays using the start of the message (case-insensitive):
     - features: messages starting with "feat"
     - fixes: messages starting with "fix"
     - docs: messages starting with "doc" or "docs"
     - other: all other messages
   - The JSON must include exactly these keys: { "features": string[], "fixes": string[], "docs": string[], "other": string[] }.

4) output/branch.txt — a single line string with a suggested branch name, computed from input/branch_description.txt:
   - Determine prefix: "fix" if the description contains any of "fix", "bug", or "patch" (case-insensitive); else "docs" if it contains "doc" (case-insensitive); else "feature".
   - Create a slug from the description by lowercasing, replacing any sequence of non-alphanumeric characters with a single hyphen, truncating to 50 characters, then trimming leading/trailing hyphens.
   - Output exactly: "<prefix>/<slug>" (no extra spaces). A trailing newline is acceptable.

Important:
- Treat the provided input files as authoritative stand-ins for the actual CLI outputs; do not run any external commands.
- Ensure all JSON is strictly valid (double-quoted keys and strings) and written to the exact output/ paths listed.
- Do not write anything outside of the output/ directory.
- Do not use network access or external tools.

Please confirm completion by writing these four files:
- output/commit.json
- output/pr.json
- output/changelog.json
- output/branch.txt
